extra.reasoning:
The property requires globally (opted_out implies not sent). Transition t5 directly violates this: "on send_attempt if (opted_out and ticks >= 8): sent := true". This sets sent=true while opted_out remains true.

Reachability of t5's guard: Start at (opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=0). Fire 'optout' (t1) -> opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=0. Fire 'tick' (t4) eight times -> ticks increments 0->1->...->8 (capped at 8), each setting sent=false; result (opted_out=true, sent=false, ticks=8). Now fire 'send_attempt': t2's guard (not opted_out) is false; t3's guard (opted_out) is true and t5's guard (opted_out and ticks>=8) is also true, so both t3 and t5 are enabled simultaneously (nondeterministic conflict). Taking t5 yields (opted_out=true, sent=true, ticks=8), which violates opted_out implies not sent.

Event count = 1 (optout) + 8 (tick) + 1 (send_attempt) = 10, within the 12-event bound. Therefore the property is violated.
